rulings.Case Title: SHAHIN ZARAFSHAR ET AL VS. KWONG SANG INVESTMENT, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 3, 2022 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer ot the first and the fourth causes of action is OVERRULED. These causes of action are sufficiently stated, including the nature of the contract. Demurrer to the cause of action for unjust enrichment is sustained with leave to amend for the cross-complaint to allege the nature of the amounts/benefits that have been received and to be disgorged/restored; and to allege a quasi-contract claim separate and apart from the express contract, which appears to be the basis for liability in the operative cross complaint (paragraph 24). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 2B LIVING, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JESSICA LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 3, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (FILED 9/28/2022) is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift their burden and show that defendant is entitled to a judgment on the only issue listed the Notice to Motion (Covid rent). This case arises out of rent demand after March 31, 2022. To the extent Defendant is seeking summary judgment on the additional ground re: outstanding NOV, this is not listed in the Notice of Motion and is not supported by competent admissible evidence. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
